plain_definition: The US federal agency responsible for administering the federal securities laws -- registration and disclosure by issuers, and licensing and supervision of broker-dealers, investment advisers, transfer agents, and national securities exchanges. A crypto asset that meets the definition of a 'security' (for example, under the Howey test, as a 'digital asset security') falls within SEC jurisdiction. Since March 2026 the SEC has jointly issued, with the CFTC, an interpretation setting out a taxonomy for classifying crypto assets, and leads 'Project Crypto,' its initiative to update SEC rules for crypto assets and on-chain markets, which the SEC and CFTC have since pursued jointly on some workstreams.
formal_definition: Established under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Press releases: sec.gov/news/pressreleases.rss (per this radar's watcher configuration). SEC Release Nos. 33-11412; 34-105020, 'Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets,' issued jointly with the CFTC and effective March 23, 2026.
